name: Nissan 370Z
reliability: 7/10 — strong VQ engine, but the clutch slave cylinder failure is a real black mark
popular_mods: Intake, high-flow cats / test pipes, catback exhaust
Uprev or Ecutek ECU tune (modest NA gains)
Forced induction (turbo/supercharger kits) for serious power
Coilovers, sway bars, big brake kit

generations.350Z (Z33, 2003–2008): Predecessor. 3.5L VQ35 V6 (287–306hp). The car that revived the Z nameplate in the US.
generations.370Z (Z34, 2009–2020): 3.7L VQ37VHR, 332hp. World's first manual with SynchroRev Match. Long 12-year run with minimal changes. Nismo trim made 350hp.
generations.Nissan Z (RZ34, 2023–present): Successor. 3.0L twin-turbo VR30DDTT, 400hp (shared with Q50 Red Sport). Dropped the displacement-based name.

common_issues: Concentric slave cylinder (CSC) failure — cheap plastic part, requires transmission removal ($1,500-$3,500)
Oil consumption (shared VQ trait) at higher mileage
Rear tire wear from aggressive factory camber
maintenance_tips: Replace the plastic CSC with an aftermarket metal unit proactively
Use Nissan ester oil and check level regularly
Get an alignment to slow rear tire wear
Let the gearbox warm up before hard 2nd-gear shifts
